localizations.title: The Tonipal's Treasure
localizations.hiddenObject_1: Did you take a close look at the floor of the jail?
localizations.hiddenObject_2: Did you look at the harbor's sign. It doesn't show a distance, but a map.
localizations.hiddenObject_3: Did you take a look at the plantation's address? Go there!
localizations.code_1025: The door is unlocked! Tonipal Island is all yours.
localizations.code_1111_dial: Too late! You must fight now!
localizations.code_1111: Yeah… I'm gonna check what you just told me. Don't go anywhere.
localizations.code_1603: The ground shakes and the grave pivots, revealing a coffin.
localizations.code_2510: The door is unlocked! Tonipal Island is all yours.
localizations.code_5643: The guard is killed. You enter the master's office.
localizations.code_6662: You misfired. Luckily, for that matter, as you were aiming at the wrong ship. The gunners lose time reloading.
localizations.code_6663: You misfired. Luckily, for that matter, as you were aiming at the wrong ship. The gunners lose time reloading.
localizations.code_6664: The Salty Peril has been hit! But Johnson escapes on a rowboat.
localizations.code_6665: You misfired. Luckily, for that matter, as you were aiming at the wrong ship. The gunners lose time reloading.
localizations.code_6666: You misfired. Luckily, for that matter, as you were aiming at the wrong ship. The gunners lose time reloading.
localizations.code_6667: You misfired. Luckily, for that matter, as you were aiming at the wrong ship. The gunners lose time reloading.
localizations.code_8462: The music box opens! The treasure is within reach. Time is limited.
localizations.code_9937: You have found the treasure! The adventure stops here… unless…
localizations.hint_53_first: Use the key and prop it up to open the chest.
localizations.hint_53: For each line of the chest, place the key facing the white arrows: each nick will give you a letter.
localizations.hint_56: Six boats… Only one of them is to be sunk, that of Johnson. Investigate and give the right code to the gunners!
localizations.hint_59: This bowl may contain several preparations… A voodoo potion, for instance.
localizations.hint_60: The fisherman living in this house has sealed his windows cracks with a page of an old fencing handbook.
localizations.hint_64_first: You should find a way to take a look at the lock on the other side of the door.
localizations.hint_64: The mirror reflects. Use it to see the external side of the door.
localizations.hint_65: This parrot will not say a word before he gets his goddamned biscuit.
localizations.hint_66_first: The colors point to the ingredients to be used and the animals to the units of measure.
localizations.hint_67: These needles can only be used with the voodoo doll. Wait for the right moment.
localizations.hint_70: Watch closely the movements of your adversaries and try to find the right parry. You must have seen them somewhere on a window.
localizations.hint_75_first: Each stone can be placed on the compass watch.
localizations.hint_75: Once the stones are placed, each color will match a number which will give you the code.
localizations.hint_77_first: Doesn't the parrot's sentence match a rebus? It will be useful sooner or later…
localizations.hint_84_first: You should target the undamaged organs of a crippled man to make him harmless.
localizations.hint_84: Target the right parts of the doll using the needles.
localizations.hint_90_first: Press the right number of times each lever or button and add half of the available sugar canes.
localizations.hint_90: You need 6 kilos of sugar cane to make 100 centiliters (i.e. 1 liter) of rum.
localizations.hint_95: This potion would rise you from the dead... Well, it's a figure of speech (hopefully).
localizations.hint_98_first: You have nothing to rotate the cross-headed screws...
localizations.hint_98: Use the piece of eight for the flat screws, it should be enough to weaken the lock.
localizations.hint_105_first: Use the voodoo doll and the needles!
localizations.hint_105: You should target Johnson's undamaged organs to make him harmless.
localizations.hint_114_first: The sun rises to the East. East = 3.
localizations.hint_114: If East = 3, then West = 9. Each colored stone matches a number.
localizations.hint_116: This newspaper contains many precious information, yesterday's date and Mami Wata's address, for instance.
localizations.hint_117: Take the cards as indicated in the discard pile and look at their backs… Then, reconstruct the puzzle.
localizations.hint_119: Each leg represents an ingredient measure. Then, follow the colors of each animal.
localizations.hint_124_first: Take the first letter of each word and you'll obtain the code in Roman numerals. The newspaper's date will also be useful.
localizations.hint_136: There are 4 spaces in which stones can be lodged. You must find them and place them on the compass. Each will provide a number.
localizations.hint_139: Johnson's boat has at least 2 cannon ready to be fired. So, one ship less.
localizations.hint_143_first: The code is simple and changes every day. What changes every morning?
localizations.hint_143: According to this newspaper, it was October 24 yesterday… So, today is…
localizations.hint_146: You must go to the plantation. The address is in the letter's header. Take the map.
localizations.hint_152: Johnson's boat comes from the East, that is from the right side. So, one ship less.
localizations.hint_157_first: Take a closer look at the tattoo, it will lead you to the cove.
localizations.hint_157: The anchor is an addition. Once solved, it will point you to the cove.
localizations.hint_160: Johnson soaks his sails in his victims' blood. So, his sails have a particular color.
localizations.hint_165: A key will give you the means to open this chest.
localizations.hint_3: This cur knows Tonipal Island by heart: show him a map of the island and he will guide you.
localizations.hint_4: You must find the potion that will wake Mami Wata up… You still have to find the recipe.
localizations.hint_5: There's nothing to buy here… But this piece of eight can be used to unscrew something.
localizations.hint_7: Look at the graffiti on this wall… What's the color of the hull of Johnson's boat?
localizations.hint_9: You should clean this window with a cloth to see something.
localizations.hint_12: No one would eat this except for an animal!
localizations.hint_13: You must go to the harbour… Look at the sign: it will show you which card to take.
localizations.hint_18_first: There are 12 kilos of sugar cane. Don't you think it's a bit much?
localizations.hint_18: Use only half of the sugar cane. So, the number on the card (18) becomes… add it to the result of the alembic.
localizations.hint_21: Dots on the horizon: use a spyglass to better see what's going on.
localizations.hint_24: (Bad) Rum. Barely good for the captive pirates.
localizations.hint_28: To fight, grab a saber! But if you parley, you may be able to avoid the fight.
localizations.hint_29: This pirate will only help you if you give him a bottle of rum. Don't even try giving him anything else.
localizations.hint_34: With this mirror, you can see the other side of the door.
localizations.hint_35: Maybe you'll be able to see the horizon with this spyglass.
localizations.hint_38: You must look for the voodoo witch. Her coordinates are a symbol in the newspaper. The dog will guide you.
localizations.hint_42: This saber is for fighting only… Given its condition, let's hope the enemy is a poor fighter.
localizations.hint_43_first: Listen to the parrot: "EYE" "CAN" "KNOT" "SEA" "U"… It will help you put the poem back in sequence.
localizations.hint_43: EYE CAN KNOT SEA U! Read the poem in this sequence.
localizations.hint_51: Use this cloth to clean something very dirty. Like a window.
localizations.tonipal_dialog_4_question: Yeah… And who is he working for?
